data.title: Xcaret Global: 8 Epic Eco-Parks in Mexico's Riviera Maya
data.excerpt: Discover Xcaret Global, Mexico's premier eco-theme park collection. Nature, culture, and adventure collide across 8 unforgettable parks in the Riviera Maya.

$media.alt: Xcaret Global: 8 Epic Eco-Parks in Mexico's Riviera Maya

children.text: What Is Xcaret Global?

Xcaret Global is the powerhouse brand behind Mexico's most celebrated collection of eco-theme parks, anchored in the heart of the Riviera Maya and Cancún region. Forget your typical amusement park with roller coasters and cotton candy—Xcaret Global built something radically different: immersive experiences that weave together raw nature, ancient Mayan culture, and genuine adventure into a single booking.

The company operates eight distinct parks, each with its own personality. Some are gentle and exploratory. Others will have you rappelling into underground rivers at midnight. What ties them all together is a commitment to showcasing Mexico's biodiversity and heritage without dumbing it down or sanitizing it into boredom.
When you book through
Xcaret's official site
, you're not buying a ticket—you're buying a day (or several days) that will rewire your expectations of what a theme park can be.
The Eight Parks: A Quick Tour of Each
Let me walk you through the lineup, because understanding what each park offers is the key to building your perfect trip.
Xcaret Park

is the flagship. It sits on 200+ acres of jungle, underground rivers, coral reefs, and a Mexican folk art museum. The evening show—"Xcaret México Espectacular"—features 300 performers tracing Mexico's history from pre-Hispanic times through today. I've seen people cry during this show. Not exaggerating.
Xel-Há

bills itself as the world's largest natural aquarium, and that's not marketing fluff. The inlet where fresh cenote water meets the Caribbean creates a snorkeling paradise teeming with tropical fish. All-inclusive food and drinks are part of your entry. You can literally float for hours on an inner tube through mangrove channels, stopping for ceviche whenever you feel like it.
Xplor

is where your adrenaline gets its money's worth. Think 13 zip-lines (some reaching 45 meters high), amphibious vehicles on jungle trails, rafting through illuminated underground rivers, and hammock-splashing into cenotes. The park runs both day and night versions—the nighttime iteration,
Xplor Fuego
, adds fire elements and torch-lit circuits that feel cinematic.
Xavage

targets the action-hungry crowd with white-water rafting, monster truck driving, rope courses, and jet boat rides. If Xplor is adventure with a scenic backdrop, Xavage is adventure with the volume turned to eleven.
Xoximilco

recreates Mexico City's famous floating gardens with colorful trajinera boats, live mariachi bands, open bar, and traditional food. It's essentially a floating party through canals at sunset. Pure joy.
Xenses

is a half-day sensory park that messes with your perception in the best way possible. You'll walk through a town that appears upside down, float in a salt-rich river without trying, and navigate a pitch-dark cave using only sound and touch. Quirky and genuinely fun.
Xenotes

takes you to four different cenotes—each with a distinct character. One is open-air with sunlight pouring in. Another is a dark cavern. You kayak, rappel, snorkel, and zip-line through all four. It's a full-day cenote crawl.
Xcaret Xailing
(the newest addition) offers catamaran excursions to Isla Mujeres with snorkeling stops and beach time. It bridges the gap between park experience and Caribbean sailing adventure.
You can explore
all parks and combo options here
to find the right mix for your group.
New & Featured Experiences Worth Highlighting
Xcaret Global doesn't sit still. They constantly refine and add to their offerings, which is part of why repeat visitors keep coming back.
The
multi-day combo passes

are a game-changer. Instead of buying individual park tickets, you can bundle two, three, four, or even all parks at steep discounts. The "Xcaret Total" pass includes access to all eight parks plus Hotel Xcaret México's all-inclusive amenities. For families or groups spending a week in the Riviera Maya, the math works out remarkably well.
Hotel Xcaret México

itself deserves attention. Rated 4.6 out of 5 on TripAdvisor with nearly 16,000 reviews, this all-inclusive resort includes unlimited access to Xcaret parks as part of your stay. Yes, you read that correctly—your hotel booking comes with park admission. The resort features oceanfront suites, a private beach, multiple restaurants (including a stunning rooftop Japanese-Mexican fusion spot), and a spa that draws from indigenous wellness traditions. The concept of "All-Fun Inclusive" means your transportation to and from parks, your meals inside the parks, and your activities are all covered.
Check current rates and availability for Hotel Xcaret México

The parks have also invested heavily in sustainability. Xcaret Park runs one of Mexico's most successful sea turtle conservation programs, releasing over 1 million hatchlings since inception. Their coral reef restoration project in Xel-Há is actively rebuilding damaged reef structures. When you visit, you're financially supporting conservation at scale—something that feels significantly better than handing money to a generic entertainment conglomerate.
For those interested in the logistics of planning, our
step-by-step guide to using Xcaret Global
breaks down booking flows, transportation, and timing strategies that most visitors miss.
Who Is Xcaret Global Ideal For?
This is where I get specific, because Xcaret Global isn't trying to be everything for everyone—and that focus is exactly what makes it excellent.
Families with kids ages 4-16

will find Xcaret Global almost unfairly well-suited. Xcaret Park and Xel-Há have shallow water areas, child-friendly trails, and educational programs disguised as fun. The evening shows captivate all ages. Xenses is a hit with curious kids who love "weird" experiences. And because Hotel Xcaret México is all-inclusive, parents don't have to budget every meal and activity separately.
Couples and honeymooners

should look at Xplor Fuego for a memorable date night (zip-lining through fire-lit jungle is hard to top), Xoximilco for a festive dinner-on-the-water vibe, and the hotel's adults-only sections for downtime.
Adventure seekers and active travelers

will gravitate toward Xavage and Xplor. The zip-line circuits at Xplor are legitimately thrilling—not the tame 20-second rides you find at beach tourist traps. Xavage's white-water rafting and monster truck driving offer a level of intensity that usually requires visiting separate specialized outfitters.
Culture enthusiasts

will be drawn to Xcaret Park's archaeological replicas, the Mexican folk art museum, the butterfly pavilion, and the evening cultural show. It's one of the few places where you can snorkel a cenote in the morning and watch a pre-Hispanic ball game reenactment in the afternoon.
Groups celebrating milestones

—birthdays, reunions, bachelor/bachelorette parties—will appreciate Xoximilco's party atmosphere and the combo pass flexibility that lets each person in the group pursue different interests during the day.
Our detailed breakdown of
Xcaret Global use cases
helps match specific parks to specific trip goals.
How to Get the Most Out of Your Xcaret Experience
A few practical insights that separate a good Xcaret trip from an unforgettable one:
Book combo passes early.
The multi-park bundles are cheaper when purchased in advance online, and some parks (especially Xel-Há and Xplor) hit capacity during peak season. Our
tips and best practices guide
covers optimal booking windows and seasonal pricing patterns.
Start with Xcaret Park on your first day.
It's the most comprehensive introduction to the brand and sets context for everything else. The evening show is the perfect capstone to day one.
Dedicate full days to single parks.
Trying to rush through Xel-Há in a half-day or hitting two parks in one afternoon leads to FOMO and exhaustion. Each park is designed for a full day of exploration.
Use the hotel shuttle system.
If you're staying at Hotel Xcaret México, the included transportation eliminates the biggest logistical headache. Buses run frequently and on schedule.
Bring a waterproof phone pouch.
You will be in water—a lot. Underground rivers, cenotes, snorkeling lagoons, water slides. A $10 waterproof pouch saves you from a $1,000 phone replacement.
Eat inside the parks.
The food quality at Xcaret parks is genuinely good—fresh ceviche, cochinita pibil tacos, tropical fruit stations. If you have the all-inclusive pass, there's no reason to leave for meals.
For comparisons with other popular project and travel planning tools, you might find our guides on
monday.com
or
ClickUp
useful for organizing your broader travel itinerary.
Why Xcaret Global Stands Apart

The travel industry is full of attractions that promise "experiences" and deliver mediocre photo ops. Xcaret Global avoids this trap because the company was built by people who genuinely care about Mexico's natural and cultural wealth.

The parks aren't afterthoughts bolted onto a hotel brand. The parks came first—the hotel was built to complement them. That origin story shows in every detail: the conservation programs, the living ecosystems within park boundaries, the cultural authenticity of performances, and the infrastructure that handles thousands of daily visitors without feeling overcrowded.

When you compare it to other Riviera Maya attractions, Xcaret Global's breadth is unmatched. No other operator offers eight distinct parks covering nature, culture, adventure, sensory play, and nightlife under one booking umbrella. The combo pass model alone makes it the smartest value proposition for anyone spending more than three days in the region.
And the reviews speak volumes. Nearly 16,000 reviews for Hotel Xcaret México at a 4.6 rating isn't a fluke—it's consistent quality at scale. Our deeper analysis of
why to choose Xcaret Global
explores the competitive advantages in more detail.
Ready to Experience Xcaret Global?

If you've made it this far, you already know this isn't a hard sell. Xcaret Global sells itself once you understand what's included and how the parks interconnect. The combination of nature immersion, cultural depth, real adventure, and all-inclusive convenience is genuinely rare in the global travel landscape.
Whether you're planning a family vacation, a romantic getaway, or an adventure-packed week with friends,
start exploring Xcaret Global's park combos and hotel packages here
. The booking process is straightforward, combo discounts are substantial, and you'll walk away with memories that last far longer than any souvenir.
*Disclosure: TonyCiencia may earn a commission if you book through our links, at no extra cost to you. We only recommend experiences we genuinely believe are worth your time and money.*

children.text: How many parks are included in Xcaret Global?

Xcaret Global operates eight parks in the Riviera Maya and Cancún area: Xcaret Park, Xel-Há, Xplor, Xavage, Xoximilco, Xenses, Xenotes, and Xcaret Xailing. Each park focuses on a different mix of nature, culture, and adventure. Combo passes let you bundle multiple parks at discounted rates.
Is Hotel Xcaret México all-inclusive with park access?

Yes. Hotel Xcaret México operates on an 'All-Fun Inclusive' model, meaning your stay includes unlimited access to Xcaret parks, meals inside the parks, and round-trip transportation. It's rated 4.6 out of 5 on TripAdvisor with nearly 16,000 reviews and features oceanfront suites, multiple restaurants, and a full spa.
Which Xcaret park is best for families with young children?

Xcaret Park and Xel-Há are the top picks for families with young children. Xcaret offers shallow water areas, a butterfly pavilion, a children's playground, and educational exhibits. Xel-Há features calm inlet waters perfect for beginner snorkeling and floating river sections. Both parks include food and have well-maintained facilities for families.
